digest.component_or_area: plan-review kernel (Pass-2 verification / Pass-3 deterministic decide)
propositions: A blocking finding survived Pass-2 on a false absence premise even though a sibling advisory in the same verdict quoted the provision it claimed was missing.

Add abstain-safe binaries claims_absence and absence_confirmed_in_context to the shared verification contract, and a symmetric Pass-3 veto that drops a finding when it claims absence but the provision is confirmed present.

DET-tier findings can be contentless (no location, no evidence spans), so add a deterministic hygiene backstop at the DET emission/aggregation point that drops subject-less DET findings without touching LLM-tier findings.
An audit confirms the P5 task-dag sibling-overlap emitter already names path and ticket subjects, so it is not a fix target; the backstop is the enforceable guarantee.
DET-detect the [operator-attested] AC tag and inject an operator_attested flag into severity_attributes, clearing ac_unverifiable upstream so impact_plan math stays byte-unchanged.
Findings against operator-attested ACs lose only the ac_unverifiable hard-override contribution while surviving on any other axis; rubrics and one new coaching move gain ADR-0043 vocabulary.
